_description: AMAS Conflict Resolution Example: Same-Tier Conflict (Rules 2-5)
_scenario: Two Tier 2 (SESSION) sources provide contradictory information. The user uploads a document stating a budget of $50,000, then later states in conversation that the budget is $75,000. Rules 2 and 3 resolve the conflict.

object_a.content: Project budget: $50,000. Approved by finance committee 2026-02-15.

object_b.content: Actually the budget was revised to $75,000 last week.

resolution.reasoning: Both objects are Tier 2, so Rule 1 does not resolve. MO-USR-023 explicitly supersedes MO-DOC-015 (the supersedes field is set). The user has authority at Tier 2 to supersede other Tier 2 content. The supersession chain is traceable. If the supersedes field were absent, Rule 3 (recency) would also favor MO-USR-023 as the more recent source.
resolution.action: Use $75,000 as the operative budget figure. Retain MO-DOC-015 in the record with a note that it was superseded. The supersession is logged for audit.

_fallback_note: If neither object had a supersedes field and both had identical timestamps, Rule 4 (specificity) would apply — the more specific claim ('revised to $75,000 last week') would prevail over the general document. If still unresolved, Rule 5 (conservative default) would flag the conflict for operator resolution.
